title: Transactional Outbox, Inbox, and Sagas · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model with one stale generation, blocked notification, goroutine leak, acknowledgement, route, or effect defect; locate cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Dual Write Failure Matrix
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go messaging team handles case 3199 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then reuse a message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Inspect the Dual Write Failure Matrix trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Dual Write Failure Matrix.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Dual Write Failure Matrix, keep Dual Write Failure Matrix connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.
